mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in over 60% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable consumer price indices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative forward returns in over 60% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strengthening corporate output capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in over 50% of expansion-phase observations or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP demonstrates sustained macroeconomic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of positive GDP growth quarters over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market, pushing S&P 500 forward returns higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models or yields negative returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0% annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 60% of high-inflation triggered instances or signal loses predictive power post-2022.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in housing starts during a post-recession recovery phase signals renewed consumer confidence and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal generates negative returns in over 50% of recovery-phase observations or Rank IC remains below 0.03.
